methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

data.narrative:
Stoke Space is a Series D-stage space and aerospace company founded by Andy Lapsa and Tom Feldman, currently valued at $1.0B post-money on $860M raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies in the same sector and stage cohort, plus 5 publicly traded space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two methodologies are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.5x multiplier typical for Series D-to-next-stage advancement, implies a $1.5B valuation. Peer-median triangulation across 8 same-sector and stage-adjacent companies—including Astranis ($3.5B, Series D), ICEYE ($2.8B, Series E), and Axiom Space ($2.5B, Series C)—yields a $1.6B median, with a range of $1.0B to $3.5B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (53.2x sector median across 6 tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated range supported by these two methods is $1.3B to $1.9B, with a median of $1.6B. This implies modest upside from the current $1.0B valuation. Key uncertainties include wide dispersion within the private peer set (particularly Astranis outlier at $3.5B), lack of revenue visibility to anchor public comparables, and stage-specific investor appetite within frontier aerospace.
